summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: JPMorgan revenue rises on payments, card income
articles.description: The largest U.S. bank benefited from corporate and consumer demand for its services in the first quarter despite macroeconomic turmoil.

articles.title: Warren pounds X Money plans
articles.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren demanded information from billionaire Elon Musk about his plans to launch a digital wallet this month.

articles.title: Stablecoins remain little used for payments
articles.description: Less than one percent of stablecoins are used for payments, and about a fifth aren’t being used at all, a Kansas City Fed researcher estimated.

articles.title: DailyPay pushes back against NY AG
articles.description: The earned wage access provider says its relationship with employers means it’s not a consumer lender, as the New York attorney general alleges.

articles.title: Which Discover jobs are disappearing
articles.description: As Capital One integrates the card business, it’s eliminating more than 100 application engineer jobs.

articles.title: Expensive tabs shift to mobile: Stripe
articles.description: Digital wallets are driving a migration of more expensive purchases from desktop to mobile devices, a Stripe report found.

articles.title: Repay faces Kubra purchase opposition
articles.description: A major shareholder of the digital bill payment services provider bashed its plan to buy a bill payment peer.

articles.title: Adyen tool speeds business payments
articles.description: Financial operations at global enterprises remain “stubbornly complex,” as treasury teams juggle multiple banking and payment systems, the company said.
